firm.name: FTS Consulting
firm.oneLiner: FTS Consulting provides M&A services for SMEs in Switzerland, with a focus on company sales, business valuation, fit-to-sell optimization, and succession planning.

evidence: "Einloggen Datenraum" and "Plattform zur Verwaltung von Dokumenten"

fitNotes: Clear fit for an AI-native deal platform because they run SME sell-side M&A and valuation work.
They explicitly mention a data room and document management platform, which suggests deal workflow tooling matters.
Their fit-to-sell and valuation content indicates a process-heavy advisory model where automation could help.
The site does not state a larger team or multiple offices, so the operating scale appears compact.

signals.title: Published M&A interview with Markus Huber
signals.detail: The site features an interview dated 04.11.2024 about success factors in M&A transactions.

signals.title: Published 2025 guide to selling a company in Switzerland
signals.detail: The site has a 2025 article on company sales and transaction preparation.

signals.title: Published fit-to-sell optimization content
signals.detail: The site promotes a fit-to-sell approach for improving sale readiness and transaction value.

play.angle: Lead with the fact that FTS Consulting already runs a client portal and data room, which makes them a strong fit for a cleaner, more modern deal workflow. Position Fintalo as a way to replace the email, Excel, and scattered-document sprawl around buyer management, staged document release, and deal tracking, rather than as another generic CRM. Given their small team and advisory breadth, the message should be about saving partner time and tightening confidentiality, not about adding process overhead. Avoid over-indexing on AI buzzwords, and avoid implying they need to change how they advise clients, since their current process already signals some digital maturity.

objections.objection: We already have a data room and client portal, so we do not need another platform.
objections.counter: That is exactly why this can be a fit. Fintalo is not just storage, it adds buyer management, stage-gated release, outreach, and deal intelligence around the data room so the team spends less time coordinating manually.

objections.objection: We are a small team, so we do not want a heavy implementation.
objections.counter: Understood. Fintalo is meant to reduce process load quickly, and a short demo can show whether it would save time without changing your current advisory workflow.

objections.objection: Confidentiality is critical, and new tools can create risk.
objections.counter: That concern is valid. The platform is built for controlled document access and secure collaboration, so the discussion can focus on whether it improves control versus email-based coordination.

openingEmail.subject: A quick idea for your deal workflow
openingEmail.body:
Hi Laura,

I noticed FTS Consulting already offers a client portal and data room. Fintalo helps M&A advisors keep that same controlled environment, while also handling buyer management, staged document release, outreach, and deal tracking in one place.

If it is useful, I would be glad to show you a short demo and see whether it could save your team time on live mandates.

Best,
openingEmail.groundedFact: FTS Consulting already offers a client portal and data room.
